mentions.note: Expanding to CPU manufacturing amid AI agent acceleration driving massive CPU demand; CEO Rene Haas demonstrates conviction; stock justified from $115 to $172 purchase to $302 as CPU needs surge.
Record $11B quarterly revenue; Agent Force exceeding $1B; aggressive $27B buyback at cheap 12x earnings valuation; customer wins (CVS, UCLA Health) validate AI platform expansion.
CEO Michael Dell's compelling AI and accelerated computing growth story was too good to pass up; missed opportunity from $156 to $305; hardware winner in new era.
Fiber technology displacing copper in data center connectivity and within-chip interconnects; Nvidia's $3.2B strategic investment validates thesis; justified at $77 entry despite earlier rally.
Weak housing market limits upside (SSS +0.6%); owned as Federal Reserve rate-cut hedge; held up better than expected but needs Fed cuts to rally meaningfully.
Outperforming Home Depot due to DIY customer exposure; still pressured by weak housing market but doing better than expected given environment.
CEO Ynon Kreiz buying shares; stock sells at 11x earnings; toy business out of favor but bottoming; insider conviction provides strong buy signal.
CEO Matt Murphy's conviction backed by personal stock purchases; data center business rock solid despite market noise; monster Q1 results validate; stock rallied from $88 to $218.

Stagnant price while AI-enabled peers explode creates frustrating opportunity cost; thought to be cheap but proving value trap; pending sell decision if Q2 disappoints; better AI beneficiaries elsewhere.

Exceptional 48-day march to $1T market cap understandable (HBM shortage real, demand sensational) but historically boom-bust memory industry will crash when production capacity added; too risky at current levels.
Requires housing turnover to thrive; not attractive in weak housing market; Berkshire Hathaway's recent selling validates weakness.
17% revenue growth; 17% DTC SSS (vs 8.5% expected); 25-cent earnings beat; brand on fire; CEO Patrice Louvet's execution stellar on women's apparel and accessories expansion; near all-time highs.
Strong off-price execution benefiting from consumer trade-down; TJX and Ross separating via superior management execution in weak consumer environment.
Stock bottoming amid AI uncertainties; CEO Bill McDermott respected but software plays lagging hardware in accelerated computing theme; will recover but not preferred.
33% revenue growth; 38% RPO growth; AI business booming; $6B AWS partnership and Natoma acquisition position as control plane for enterprise AI; reframes from software to consumption platform.
Turnaround story with 32% earnings growth; SSS +5.6% (vs 2.4% expected); raised full-year outlook; cheap at 15x earnings with 3.6% yield; home run quarter.
6% SSS (vs 4.1% expected); 9% revenue growth; HomeGoods +9%; 29% earnings growth; raised guidance and buyback; off-price kingpin thriving as consumers trade down in K-shaped recovery.
Matched SSS expectations (4.1%) but sky-high valuation; management cited new consumer pressure; 7.2% pullback is rare buying opportunity if consumer stabilizes.
